description: Checkpoint verification MUST fail when the signature does not verify over the recomputed checkpoint preimage (RFC-ACDP-0012 §9.3 step 2). This fixture takes the genuine log-001 checkpoint and alters root_hash AFTER signing (first hex nibble flipped from '0' to 'f') while keeping the original signature bytes — the shape of a history rewrite that reuses an old signature. JCS-recomputing the preimage over the served object yields a different checkpoint hash, so the pinned signature MUST fail to verify. The analogue of rcpt-002 one layer up: the failure is detected by recomputation, never by trusting served bytes.

input.scenario: Consumer calls GET /log/checkpoint (or receives log_inclusion.log_checkpoint) and verifies the checkpoint per RFC-ACDP-0012 §9.3 against the registry's DID document.

expected.behavior: RFC-ACDP-0012 §9.3 step 1 passes (closed parse, checkpoint_version 'acdp-log/1'). Step 2 MUST fail: JCS-canonicalizing the served checkpoint minus 'signature' and hashing yields a checkpoint hash ≠ input.genuine_checkpoint_hash, and the pinned signature does not verify over its ASCII bytes under the registry receipt key. The checkpoint MUST be rejected with the invalid_log_proof category; every proof presented against it is thereby unverified. A verifier that byte-compares root_hash against a served value instead of recomputing-and-verifying the signature would wrongly accept this checkpoint.
expected.evidence_note: Contrast the split-view case: TWO checkpoints that each verify under §9.3 but fail a §9.2 consistency check between them are not a verification error — they are valid, non-repudiable evidence of registry misbehavior, and consumers SHOULD retain both (RFC-ACDP-0012 §15).
expected.verdict_independence: The body, registry_receipt, and lineage_head_receipt verdicts are independent and unaffected (RFC-ACDP-0012 §9.3).
